water.name: Sweetwater River and tributaries upstream of Sweetwater Reservoir

authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(151) — Sweetwater River and tributaries upstream of Sweetwater Reservoir (San Diego Co.)
sources.quotedText: (151) Sweetwater River and tributaries upstream of Sweetwater Reservoir (San Diego Co.). All year. Only artificial lures may be used. 2 trout.

sources.title: Cuyamaca State Park Fishing — San Diego & SoCal Fishing Forums
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 273985, "Sweetwater River", Stream, CA, San Diego Co.
sources.quotedText: GNIS has no reservoir-specific entry for Sweetwater Reservoir; location corroborated via Wikipedia's infobox coordinate for Sweetwater Reservoir (32.69694,-116.98722, sourced from GNIS ID 273984) and cross-checked against OSM/Nominatim's "Sweetwater Reservoir" way centroid (32.6986736,-116.98379); anchored to the Sweetwater River's GNIS stream record (gaz_id 273985); correcting the file's prior coordinate (-116.6106,32.8556), which sat ~39km from the true reservoir location, up in the Cuyamaca Mountains headwaters rather than near the reservoir the water's name is anchored to.

parameters.note: Only artificial lures may be used; live or dead bait is prohibited.

regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 trout daily bag limit; possession limit equals one daily bag limit (2) per the general provision at 14 CCR §7.50(a) absent a separately stated possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
